title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Compiler Case: Overlapping Binding Analysis
summary: Routing keys encode unstable JSON, topic wildcards overlap unexpectedly, headers matching trusts absent fields, and unroutable messages vanish because mandatory and alternate behavior are not designed together. Planned artifact: Add and verify the overlapping binding analysis increment in multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Overlapping Binding Analysis · Theory Overlapping Binding Analysis
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9987 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Overlapping Binding Analysis contract for the supplied data.
